name: Asparagus Crab Soup (Sup Mang Tay Cua)
recipeIngredient: 2 Shallots or 2 Scallions, chopped white part
1/2 pound Crab meat, fresh, frozen, or canned
2 teaspoons Cornstarch dissolved in 2 tablespoons Water
recipeInstructions:
The French introduced asparagus to the Vietnamese, who promptly incorporated this classic vegetable into their cuisine. The Vietnamese word for asparagus is "Western bamboo," due to its resemblance to bamboo shoots. Asparagus is universally popular throughout Vietnam, and this light, tasty dish will delight your family.
1. Bring water to a boil and add the pork bones. Remove any scum that forms, then cover and continue boiling the bones for 1 hour. Remove the bones from the stock and discard them.
2. Add salt and fish sauce to the stock.
3. Heat oil in a pan and add chopped garlic and shallots. Add crab meat and fry for 5 minutes over high heat. Sprinkle with 1/8 teaspoon black pepper, stirring constantly, then add the crab meat mixture to the soup and bring to a boil.
4. Add cornstarch-and-water mixture and stir for a few minutes.
5. Break egg into the actively boiling soup while stirring. Cook, still stirring, for about 2 minutes.
6. Drop in asparagus along with liquid from can and remaining black pepper. Continue cooking until asparagus is heated through.
7. Sprinkle coriander and scallion greens over soup before serving.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.
